mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.
precondition_mapping.notes: This batch contains UpdatePlanPage which is for changing plans, not enrolling printers. No printer enrollment methods found. Enrollment functionality likely exists in different batches containing enrollment or setup page objects.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: UpdatePlanPage has free_trial_plan_details and free_trial_info_card locators for displaying free trial information, but no methods to verify or set subscription state to ensure no free months. These are display elements, not setup/verification methods.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: No subscription status verification methods found in this batch. UpdatePlanPage is for plan management UI, not for checking subscription status via API or backend.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: UpdatePlanPage has pause_plan_info and resume_plan_link locators, but these are for DISPLAYING pause status and RESUMING a paused plan, not for PAUSING the plan initially. No pause plan button or pause instant ink link found in this batch. The pause action likely occurs on a different page or modal not included in this batch.

test_case_mappings.no_match_reason: This test case requires components for the Print and Payment History page, specifically the Billing Cycle Period card with pause plan information, complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages progress bar, tooltips, and page usage tracking. Batch 16 contains the UpdatePlanPage which is a completely different page focused on changing subscription plans, upgrading/downgrading, and managing plan settings. While UpdatePlanPage has some pause-related display elements (pause_plan_info, resume_plan_link), these are for showing pause status on the Update Plan page, not for the Billing Cycle Period card on the Print and Payment History page. The test case requires 47 unique UI elements including billing_cycle_period_card, plan_pause_information_text, complimentary_pages_progress_bar, additional_pages_progress_bar, complimentary_pages_info_icon, complimentary_pages_tooltip, complimentary_pages_value_text, additional_pages_value_text, total_pages_printed_text, and various info messages - none of which exist in UpdatePlanPage. The test also requires navigation to Print and Payment History page via print_and_payment_history_link, which is not present in this batch. Additionally, the test requires backend time manipulation methods (shift_time_by_days, trigger_billing_charge) and print simulation methods (register_print_job) which are not present in this batch. The test data files in this batch (RTPDS payloads for printer supply status and address data) are unrelated to billing cycle display and page usage tracking. The Print and Payment History page object with all required billing cycle card elements likely exists in a different batch containing history, billing, or dashboard page objects.
